podcasts.name: Davie Fogarty
podcasts.title: The food is good… but no one is buying
podcasts.transcript: How many customers are you getting and how many do you actually need to [music] really have a good business? >> So on like a Wednesday or a Thursday, they're our quiet nights. I'd probably do on average nine pastas. >> Wow. The fact that Ash is only doing nine pastas a night is truly shocking because the food is so good. Something doesn't add up here. I'm going to have to dig a bit deeper. So what are you currently doing to get customers in the door from a marketing perspective? >> We're not marketing. >> Got you. >> Uh we're not active on our social media. We've tried in the past outsourcing our marketing to other people and found that to just be very expensive and made no difference to our sales. >> It's hard. You got so many other things to manage as well and there's just so much information out there. So let me put a strategy together for you and we'll we'll get it up. >> Yeah, I'm excited. >> Go. Ash is barely posting on social media so nobody knows that he exist. I've actually seen this exact problem before in a donut store that I rescued. I'm going to replicate the plan almost exactly. All I'm going to do is get a viral product and then I'm going to make it viral on social media to get people through the door. >> Davis, I'm not looking at what's [music] going on out there at all.
